name: Fisherman's Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Cloves garlic, peeled and minced
1 (14.5 oz) can peeled, diced tomatoes, undrained
1 (14.5 oz) can vegetable broth, plus enough water to equal 3 cups
2 lb Red potatoes, halved and sliced 1/4-inch thick
1 lb Halibut fillet, cut into 4 pieces
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 cup Prepared croutons, crushed to crumbs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic; saute for 5 minutes. Add the undrained tomatoes, fennel seeds, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Add the broth-water combination, saffron, salt, and cayenne pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes. Add potatoes, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
3. Put the halibut pieces in a steamer basket that will fit over the pot. Drizzle with a little of the cooking broth and sprinkle with pepper. Steam for 12 minutes per inch of thickness, or until the fish flakes.
4. Remove the skin from the halibut and divide into 4 soup bowls. Stir the parsley and croutons into the soup. Adjust the seasonings with salt and pepper if necessary and ladle into the bowls.
